## Source
Ticket: SA-452
Add webhook config structs for Xero & QuickBooks verification secrets

## Acceptance Criteria
<!-- source: ticket -->
- [ ] `XERO_WEBHOOK_KEY` env var parsed into `XeroWebhookConfig` struct
- [ ] `QUICKBOOKS_WEBHOOK_TOKEN` env var parsed into `QuickbooksWebhookConfig` struct
- [ ] Both configs available as `Option<T>` Rocket state
- [ ] `Debug` output redacts the secret values
- [ ] Missing env vars produce `None` (no crash, graceful degradation)
- [ ] `cargo test -p virtual_assistant_api` passes with new config tests
- [ ] `cargo fmt` passes
- [ ] `cargo clippy -p virtual_assistant_api -- -D warnings` passes

## Edge Cases
- Both env vars are optional — app must start without them
- Empty string env var treated as unconfigured (None)
- Custom `Debug` implementation must redact `webhook_key` / `webhook_token` fields while still showing the struct name and any non-secret fields

## Implementation Plan
- `libs/virtual_assistant_api/src/infra/xero_webhook_config.rs` — NEW: create `XeroWebhookConfig` struct with `webhook_key: String`, custom `Debug` (redacts the key), and `from_env()` that reads `XERO_WEBHOOK_KEY` (returns `None` if missing/empty)
- `libs/virtual_assistant_api/src/infra/quickbooks_webhook_config.rs` — NEW: create `QuickbooksWebhookConfig` struct with `webhook_token: String`, custom `Debug` (redacts the token), and `from_env()` that reads `QUICKBOOKS_WEBHOOK_TOKEN` (returns `None` if missing/empty)
- `libs/virtual_assistant_api/src/infra/mod.rs` — add `pub mod xero_webhook_config;` and `pub mod quickbooks_webhook_config;`, update `pub use` re-exports to point to the new modules instead of `accounting_webhooks`
- `libs/virtual_assistant_api/src/infra/accounting_webhooks.rs` — remove the `XeroWebhookConfig` and `QuickbooksWebhookConfig` struct definitions (they are moving to the new dedicated files; keep the file if it contains other items, otherwise delete)
- `libs/virtual_assistant_api/src/lib.rs` — update `use` imports from `infra::accounting_webhooks::XeroWebhookConfig` (etc.) to their new paths; all `manage()` calls remain the same since the struct names are unchanged

## Complexity
trivial
